text: Databases are essential tools for storing and managing data. The two main types are SQL and NoSQL. SQL databases use tables with rows and columns, which are great for structured data. Queries written in SQL allow you to retrieve, update, or delete information. Data modeling is the process of designing these tables and their relationships. Indexes are used to speed up search queries, like an index in a book. In contrast, NoSQL databases handle unstructured data, such as documents or graphs, without fixed tables. They offer flexibility but require different querying methods. Choosing between SQL and NoSQL depends on your data’s structure and how you plan to use it.
